input.prompt:
I'm adding dark mode to my .NET MAUI Android app. I created ResourceDictionary
themes and used StaticResource to bind colors in my XAML pages. When I
switch themes at runtime nothing changes — the colors stay the same. Also,
when the user toggles dark mode in Android settings my app seems to restart
completely. What am I doing wrong?

input.initialPrompt:
I'm adding dark mode to my .NET MAUI Android app. I created ResourceDictionary
themes and used StaticResource to bind colors in my XAML pages. When I
switch themes at runtime nothing changes — the colors stay the same. Also,
when the user toggles dark mode in Android settings my app seems to restart
completely. What am I doing wrong?

data.content:
Two separate issues:

**1. `StaticResource` doesn't update at runtime.**
`StaticResource` is resolved once at XAML load time. Switch to `DynamicResource` so bindings re-evaluate when the dictionary changes:

```xml
<!-- ✗ won't update -->
<Label TextColor="{StaticResource PrimaryTextColor}" />

<!-- ✓ updates when theme changes -->
<Label TextColor="{DynamicResource PrimaryTextColor}" />
```

Then swap themes by clearing and merging dictionaries:

```csharp
var mergedDicts = Application.Current.Resources.MergedDictionaries;
mergedDicts.Clear();
mergedDicts.Add(useDark ? new DarkTheme() : new LightTheme());
```

**2. Android recreates the Activity on `uiMode` change.**
By default Android destroys/recreates the Activity when dark mode toggles. Add `UiMode` to `ConfigurationChanges` in your `MainActivity.cs`:

```csharp
[Activity(
    Theme = "@style/Maui.SplashTheme",
    ConfigurationChanges = ConfigChanges.ScreenSize
                         | ConfigChanges.Orientation
                         | ConfigChanges.UiMode,  // ← add this
    LaunchMode = LaunchMode.SingleTop)]
public class MainActivity : MauiAppCompatActivity { }
```

This tells Android your app handles the change itself, preventing the restart. You can then detect the system toggle and apply your theme:

```csharp
// e.g., in App.xaml.cs or a service
var uiMode = CurrentActivity.Resources.Configuration.UiMode
             & Android.Content.Res.UiMode.NightMask;
bool isDark = uiMode == Android.Content.Res.UiMode.NightYes;
```

**TL;DR:** `StaticResource` → `DynamicResource` for runtime updates; add `ConfigChanges.UiMode` to prevent Activity recreation.
data.reasoningText:
The user has two issues with their .NET MAUI Android app related to dark mode:

1. **StaticResource vs DynamicResource**: They're using `StaticResource` to bind colors, but `StaticResource` is resolved once at load time and never updated. They need to use `DynamicResource` for runtime theme switching.

2. **App restart on Android dark mode toggle**: When the user toggles dark mode in Android settings, the app restarts. This is because Android destroys and recreates the Activity by default when configuration changes (like `uiMode`) occur. They need to handle the `uiMode` configuration change in their Android manifest or Activity attribute.


I'll walk through the specific fixes they need to implement.
